prompt:
You have the Superpowers plugin installed (v5.0.4). The design is provided below — all decisions are final.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use the `brainstorming` skill. Do NOT ask clarifying questions. The design is already approved. Go directly to `writing-plans`.

## Task
Build the Platform Server for Lobster World — a WebSocket-based event coordination server for a decentralized AI agent virtual world.

## Design (Approved — do not modify)

### Architecture
- **Fastify** HTTP server + **ws** WebSocket server on same port
- Two WebSocket endpoints:
  - `/ws/lobster` — for Social Proxy connections (lobster to platform)
  - `/ws/viewer` — for Frontend 3D client connections (platform to render events)
- All shared types imported from `@lobster-world/protocol` (already exists at `packages/protocol/src/index.ts`)

### Components

#### 1. Connection Manager
- Track connected lobsters (WebSocket + metadata)
- Track connected viewers (frontend clients)
- Handle disconnect/reconnect gracefully
- Heartbeat ping/pong (30s interval)

#### 2. Lobster Registry
- Register lobster with PublicProfile + auth token
- Track online/offline status
- Store LobsterState per connected lobster
- List all lobsters in scene

#### 3. Scene Engine
- Single scene for MVP (virtual office)
- Manage lobster positions, animations, activities
- Broadcast state updates to viewers at 10Hz (100ms interval)
- Delta updates (only send changed state)
- Handle lobster join/leave events

#### 4. Dialogue Router
- Create dialogue sessions between lobsters
- Relay messages between participants
- Track turn count and token estimates (word count * 1.3 as token proxy)
- Enforce turn budget and token budget from CircuitBreakerConfig
- End sessions on budget exceeded or participant disconnect

#### 5. Circuit Breaker
- Per-session: max turns (20), max tokens (10000), max duration (10 min)
- Per-lobster: max concurrent sessions (3), cooldown after kill (5 min)
- Semantic repeat detection: track last 3 messages per participant, compare cosine similarity of simple word-frequency vectors (no ML model needed for MVP — just bag-of-words overlap ratio). Threshold 0.85 for 3 consecutive similar messages -> kill session.
- When triggered: send dialogue_ended with reason, log to audit

#### 6. Mock Lobster Client
- Simulate 3 lobsters connecting via WebSocket
- Each has a personality/behavior pattern:
  - **Coder** — mostly works at desk (typing animation), occasionally walks to coffee area
  - **Social** — walks around, frequently initiates chats, waves at others
  - **Thinker** — alternates between thinking and typing, occasionally joins conversations
- Random behavior transitions every 5-15 seconds
- Two lobsters occasionally start a dialogue (scripted exchanges, 3-5 turns)
- Mock lobsters connect to the same server via WebSocket

#### 7. REST Endpoints (for frontend)
- GET /api/scene — current scene state (for initial load)
- GET /api/lobsters — list all registered lobsters
- GET /api/dialogues — list active dialogue sessions
- GET /api/health — health check

### Tech Stack
- Runtime: Node.js 20+
- Framework: Fastify 5.x
- WebSocket: ws library
- Dev: tsx for development, tsup for build
- Test: Vitest

### Project Structure
```
apps/server/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Entry point, server setup
│   ├── config.ts             # Constants and config
│   ├── ws/
│   │   ├── connection-manager.ts
│   │   ├── lobster-handler.ts
│   │   └── viewer-handler.ts
│   ├── engine/
│   │   ├── registry.ts
│   │   ├── scene.ts
│   │   ├── dialogue.ts
│   │   └── circuit-breaker.ts
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── routes.ts
│   └── mock/
│       └── mock-lobsters.ts
├── tests/
│   ├── registry.test.ts
│   ├── scene.test.ts
│   ├── dialogue.test.ts
│   ├── circuit-breaker.test.ts
│   └── integration.test.ts
└── .dev-progress/
```

## Context
- Monorepo root: /home/node/.openclaw/workspace-main/projects/lobster-world
- Protocol package already exists at packages/protocol/src/index.ts — import all types from there
- Currently on develop branch
- Create a feature branch feature/server-mvp from develop

## Methodology
Follow this workflow strictly:
1. Use writing-plans to create a detailed implementation plan. Save to docs/superpowers/plans/. Each task: 2-5 min, test-first, with exact file paths and verification steps. Steps use checkbox syntax for tracking.
2. The plan review loop will automatically run. Fix any issues found.
3. Use subagent-driven-development to execute the plan. Each subagent gets one task with two-stage review.
4. ALL implementation MUST follow TDD: RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR.
5. Commit after EACH task completion.
6. Use verification-before-completion before declaring done.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use the brainstorming skill. Go directly to writing-plans.

## Testing (MANDATORY)
- ALL tests run inside YOUR session.
- RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR, no shortcuts.
- Unit tests for each module (registry, scene, dialogue, circuit-breaker)
- Integration: WebSocket connections + message flow
- E2E: Start server, connect mock lobsters + viewer, verify full flow

## Code Quality Checklist
1. Import ALL types from @lobster-world/protocol — never duplicate
2. No magic values — use constants
3. TypeScript strict — no any, no ts-ignore
4. Union type: validate first, then cast
5. Run formatters before done

## Feature List Tracking (MANDATORY)
Update feature-list.md after completing each feature (status changes).

## Progress Tracking (MANDATORY)
Maintain .dev-progress/progress.md.

## Git Discipline
- Branch: feature/server-mvp from develop
- Commit after EACH task
- Descriptive messages
- Push to remote when done
